results.title: Knowledge Engineer / Semantic Expert for AI
highlights:
# Knowledge Engineer / Semantic Expert for AI
[...]
Knowledge Engineer / Semantic Expert for AI
[...]
# Knowledge Engineer / Semantic Expert for AI
[...]
Data Eng, Mgmt & Governance Associate Manager | Mid-Level | Full time
[...]
## Job Description
[...]
Accenture is helping companies use generative AI and semantic layer to reinvent their enterprise and optimize business functions for breakthrough innovation and competitive advantage. With over 1,600 professionals dedicated to generative AI, leveraging the depth and experience of more than 40,000 AI and data professionals across the company our Advanced Data & AI team brings together our Experienced Innovation, Strategic Investment, Exceptional Talent, and Power Ecosystem.
[...]
As a Knowledge Engineer, you formulate real-world problems into practical, efficient, and scalable AI and Knowledge Graph problems.
[...]
You lead a team and provide guidance to explore and implement new methodologies, model building techniques, and cutting-edge algorithms, and applying these techniques with the right architecture to solve real-world problems.
[...]
You have a deep understanding and ability to remain at the forefront of knowledge engineering, generative AI, LLM, and multi-modal models (with a focus on driving innovation by applying these techniques to new business problems, use cases, and scenarios).
[...]
As a significant part of this role, you will be justifying the value of innovative generative AI and knowledge graph approaches in the business problems, and you'll be expected to construct methodologies and data architectures that clearly demonstrate their value.
[...]
The Work:
[...]
Build Knowledge Graph solutions that transform clients’ data architecture.
[...]
Design, develop, and implement AI and semantic solutions and ensure that all the pieces work together seamlessly
[...]
Work with the project team, team leaders, project delivery leads, and client stakeholders to create stand-out Data & AI offerings powered by graph-based technologies
[...]
Make the business case for the semantic layer solution recommended to the client
[...]
Pitch in on Accenture sales efforts when needed
[...]
Continue to learn and develop cutting edge Data & AI solutions, especially agentic technologies, provide through leadership on technology trends, new opportunities and innovations, or foreseeable limitations, risks, and concerns.
[...]
Bachelor's degree or equivalent (minimum 12 years' work experience). If Associate’s Degree, must have equivalent minimum 6-year work experience
[...]
Minimum of 2 years of experience with schema design, ontology management, and Knowledge Graph curation.
[...]
Minimum of 2 years of experience in designing and developing knowledge graph solutions and graph-based machine learning models, functional and technical experience required.
[...]
Minimum of 1 end-to-end data pipeline implementation for AI applications, particularly those involving LLMs or similar models, including hands-on design and configuration
[...]
Minimum of 2 year and strong knowledge of relational databases, object stores, graph databases (e.g. Stardog, Neo4J, Amazon Neptune), and vector databases
[...]
- Minimum of 2 years of experience in Knowledge Graph technologies (e.g. RDF, SPARQL, LPG, SHACL)
[...]
2+ years of hands-on experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP)
[...]
2+ years of experience in Python, with experience in frameworks like Tensorflow, PyTorch, and tools for building ETL pipelines (e.g. Apache NiFi, Airflow)
[...]
Practical experience with NLP and/or Search techniques
[...]
Prompt engineering, and LLMs for enterprise-scale applications.
[...]
Ph.D. in Computer Science, Computer or Electrical Engineering, Mathematics, or a related field.
[...]
Broad experience in diverse ML techniques and agentic systems.
[...]
#### AI and data science jobs: Uncover new possibilities
[...]
Unlock the power of AI and data to reinvent all facets of business–responsibly.

## Job Description
[...]
Project Role : AI / ML EngineerProject Role Description : Develops applications and systems that utilize AI tools, Cloud AI services, with proper cloud or on-prem application pipeline with production ready quality. Be able to apply GenAI models as part of the solution. Could also include but not limited to deep learning, neural networks, chatbots, image processing. Must have skills : Generative AIGood to have skills : NAMinimum 5 year(s) of experience is requiredEducational Qualification : 15 years full time educationSummary: As an AI / ML Engineer, a typical day involves designing and building advanced applications and systems that leverage artificial intelligence technologies. This role requires integrating cloud-based or on-premises pipelines to ensure solutions are production-ready and scalable. The engineer works on applying generative artificial intelligence models within broader projects, which may include deep learning techniques, neural network architectures, conversational agents, and image processing tasks. Collaboration with various teams to align development efforts and maintain high-quality deliverables is a key aspect of daily activities, ensuring innovative and efficient AI-driven solutions are implemented effectively. Roles & Responsibilities: - Expected to be an SME, collaborate and manage the team to perform.- Responsible for team decisions.- Engage with multiple teams and contribute on key decisions.- Provide solutions to problems for their immediate team and across multiple teams.- Lead the design and implementation of AI-driven solutions that meet project requirements and quality standards.- Facilitate knowledge sharing and mentoring within the team to foster continuous learning and skill development.- Coordinate cross-functional efforts to ensure seamless integration of AI components with existing systems and workflows. Professional & Technical Skills: - Must To Have Skills: Proficiency in Generative AI.- Experience with deep learning frameworks and neural network design.- Strong understanding of cloud AI services and deployment pipelines.- Familiarity with chatbot development and natural language processing techniques.- Knowledge of image processing algorithms and their application in AI solutions. Additional Information: - The candidate should have minimum 5 years of experience in Generative AI.- This position is based at our Hyderabad office.- A 15 years full time education is required.

highlights:
# Responsible AI Tech Lead

Responsible AI Tech Lead

# Responsible AI Tech Lead
[...]
Responsible AI Team Lead/Consultant | Full time | Experience: 5-10 years
[...]
Job No. ATCI-5310576-S1940389 | Bengaluru | Required Skill: Generative AI
[...]
## Job Description
[...]
Project Role : Responsible AI Tech LeadProject Role Description : Ensure the ethical and responsible use of artificial intelligence (AI) technologies. Design and deploy Responsible AI solutions; align AI projects with ethical principles and regulatory requirements. Provide leadership, fosters cross-functional collaboration, and advocates for ethical AI adoption. Must have skills : Generative AIGood to have skills : Python (Programming Language), Microservices and Light Weight Architecture, Microsoft Azure DevOpsMinimum 5 year(s) of experience is requiredEducational Qualification : 15 years full time educationSummary: We are seeking an experienced AI Refinery Developer with strong expertise in AI, Generative AI, and RAG-based systems. The role involves maintaining and enhancing an existing AI application built on AI Refinery (AIR), developed using FastAPI, and integrated within NVIDIA Omniverse, a 3D simulation and content creation platform.Roles & Responsibilities: -Maintain, enhance, and optimize the existing AI application/service built on AI Refinery (AIR). -Manage and improve AI chat functionalities that respond based on the contextual data from 3D environments/scenes. -Handle orchestrator-based tool services for manipulating and controlling 3D scene objects. -Develop and maintain RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) based answering services for queries related to historical project deliveries. -Understand, integrate, and maintain various AIR services and workflows used across the application. -Collaborate with cross-functional teams to support feature enhancements and ensure system stability. Professional & Technical Skills: Must-Have Skills -Strong proficiency in Python and FastAPI for backend development. -Hands-on experience with AI Refinery (AIR), LangChain, or similar LLM orchestration frameworks. -Experience working with databases such as SQL, NoSQL, and VectorDB. -Solid understanding of RAG workflows, LLM integrations, and context-based AI applications. -Experience in integrating AI/ML models into production environments. -Familiarity with document analysis, LLM-based chat systems, and data processing workflows.Good to Have Skills -Experience with cloud infrastructure (Azure/AWS/GCP) and DevOps practices. -Exposure to NVIDIA Omniverse, 3D tools, or simulation-based environments. -Understanding of microservices, containerization (Docker), and CI/CD pipelines. Additional Information: -Primary Skill (Accenture Standard): AI -Secondary Skills (recommended): Generative AI, Python, FastAPI, RAG, Vector Databases, LangChain, AI Refinery -Work involves close collaboration with AI, backend, and 3D pipeline teams. -Experience in NVIDIA ecosystem is a strong advantage. -Suitable for candidates interested in advanced AI workflows, 3D scene understanding, and enterprise AI solutions. -This position is based at our Bengaluru office. -A 15 years full time education is required.
[...]

results.title: AI/ML Architect - Forward Deployed Engineer · Reinvention Centers at Accenture | Canary Wharfian
highlights:
# AI/ML Architect - Forward Deployed Engineer · Reinvention Centers
[...]
**AI/ML Architect - Forward Deployed Engineer** designs, builds, and manages AI agents, data pipelines, and ML solutions in enterprise environments. Expert in LLM and agentic architectures, you'll prototype and deploy AI agents across cloud providers (Amazon Bedrock, Azure AI, Google Vertex AI) and Anthropic Claude SDK. Key responsibilities include: architecting AI agents, building LLM systems, implementing RAG patterns, training/deploying ML models, ensuring fairness, and integrating agents with enterprise systems. Requires 3+ years of experience, excellent engineering skills, and occasional travel (25-75%). Hybrid role based in Dallas, TX (3 days/week), with remote flexibility for qualified candidates in select US locations.
[...]
An AI/ML Architect with strong engineering skills and expertise in LLM and agentic architectures. You design, build, and operationalize AI agents, data pipelines, and ML solutions that run reliably in regulated enterprise environments.
[...]
You embed with clients to stand up custom AI agent frameworks across cloud providers (Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, Azure AI Foundry, Google Vertex AI Agent Builder) and the Anthropic Claude Agent SDK. You partner with stakeholders to define use cases, prototype agentic workflows, and ship production-ready agents that operate reliably.
[...]
Build LLM and agentic systems with function-calling, prompt engineering, and agent orchestration in production.
[...]
Implement RAG patterns with vector databases (FAISS, Pinecone, Chroma, Milvus) and tune embeddings for retrieval quality.
[...]
Train and deploy ML models with PyTorch, TensorFlow, MLflow, SageMaker, Azure ML, or Kubeflow.
[...]
Integrate AI agents with enterprise APIs, microservices, and workflow systems; ship via CI/CD on Azure, AWS, or GCP. Apply Responsible AI controls (fairness, bias detection, safety filters) in regulated industries.
[...]

A forward-thinking services company at the forefront of AI-native innovation. We partner with enterprise clients to create next-generation, agent-powered workflows engineered to scale in real-world settings. Our teams embed deeply with customers, orchestrating people, tools, and processes to move projects beyond experimentation into operational reality.
[...]
## As a AI Native Engineer:
[...]
- You will bring 2 years of experience building cloud‑native solutions and orchestrating cross‑functional initiatives.
[...]
## Responsibilities:
[...]
- Product Orchestration: Partner with business and technical stakeholders to frame problems, align priorities, and translate needs into orchestrated AI workflows.
[...]
- Agent Architecture & Engineering: Design and engineer enterprise-ready AI agents — including retrieval, orchestration, policy-based routing, tool invocation, evaluation harnesses, and lifecycle observability.
[...]
- AI Platform Integration: Develop abstraction layers across AI providers (Anthropic, Google, OpenAI, etc.) to enable seamless integration and enablement.
[...]
- Cloud-Native Product & Software Engineering: Apply containerization (Kubernetes, Docker), microservices, serverless, event-driven architectures, CI/CD, and observability to create and integrate scalable AI-native systems.
[...]
## We are looking for experience in
[...]
following skills:
[...]
- Minimum 2 years engineering experience with cloud-native systems (APIs, microservices, containerization, serverless).
[...]
- Deep expertise in designing and deploying agentic solutions (agents, orchestration, context engineering, RAG, workflows) in production environments.
[...]
- Strong experience with AI platforms — OpenAI, Claude, Vertex AI, plus open-source models — including building abstraction layers to manage multi-provider pipelines.
[...]
- Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or equivalent; additional AI certifications or agentic tool experience is a plus.
[...]
- You’ve served as a Product Engineer, Solutions Architect, or Agentic AI Engineer in an enterprise environment.
[...]
- You’ve defined or worked with enterprise-grade architectures for compound AI systems, orchestration frameworks, or agent registry/stream-based architectures.
[...]
- You master the AI-native paradigm — blending cloud-native with generative model architectures — optimizing for performance, modularity, and efficiency.
[...]
Accenture is a leading global professional services company, providing a broad range of services in strategy and consulting, interactive, technology and operations, with digital capabilities across all of these services. We combine unmatched experience and specialized capabilities across more than 40 industries — powered by the world’s largest network of Advanced Technology and Intelligent Operations centers. With 750,000 people serving clients in more than 120 countries, Accenture brings continuous innovation to help clients improve their performance and create lasting value across their enterprises. # Senior Banking Data & AI Manager
[...]
Job DescriptionThere is never a typical day at Accenture, but that is why we love it here! This is an extraordinary opportunity to build a rewarding career atAccenture Banking Technology Unit. Working in highly collaborative cross-border teams for world-leading clients, we will nurture your talent in an inclusive culture that values diversity.Whatever you are passionate about, make the most of it in a career with Accenture. Whether you are a strategic thinker, a digital innovator, a tech wizard, or a business problem solver, you will find an exciting career path on the cutting edge. We would love to get to know your unique blend of talents and help you explore and grow them further.Accenture Banking Technology Unit is part of the Accenture's global capability network, and we serve several clients in Nordics and globally. Banking Technology is in the intersection of business and technology, and we have pure techies and more business-oriented people in the team. You'll get access to Accenture's vast knowledge base and experienced colleagues to help you to grow to next level on your professional career.We are now looking forSenior Banking Data & AI Manager.As a manager, you will be leading Accenture teams, providing senior advisory and implementation services for our banking clients' leaders and driving our growth in industry. Your main goal is to help our clients to succeed in their initiatives - no matter whether it is on planning or execution stage. Your detailed work description and responsibilities will be determined by your engagement.Responsibilities:

- Ensure technical transformation is aligned with business goals and processes, and follows the architecture blueprints defined

# AI/ML Computational Science Sr Analyst, Accenture

May 13, 2026

Apply Now

## Apply for this job

Email *

Executive Name *

You accept our Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy

### Job Description

Accenture is hiring an AI/ML Computational Science Sr Analyst who will develop advanced Machine Learning and Data Science solutions for enterprise operations. The role requires the development of predictive models and the establishment of analytics systems along with the provision of immediate insights to multiple sectors which include Retail and Supply Chain and Finance. The candidate will work together with international teams to create data science solutions which can grow their business by using advanced artificial intelligence and analytical methods to analyze complicated data sets.

Experience: 5–8 years

Main Duties:

- Assist in developing machine learning and data science models for enterprise business solutions.
- Analyze complex datasets to generate insights supporting Retail, Supply Chain, and Finance operations.
- Design predictive analytics solutions including demand forecasting and time series analysis models.
- Collaborate with global data science teams to develop innovative AI-powered analytics platforms.
- Integrate analytics outcomes into real-time business systems to enhance operational decision-making.

Essential Qualifications:

- Five and eight years of experience in Data Science or Machine Learning jobs.
- Practical skills in Python and statistical programming and advanced analytics methods.
- Experience using data visualization tools which include Power BI and advanced Excel functions.
- Machine learning methods which consist of regression and classification and clustering and natural language processing.
- Handle extensive data sets while using analytical methods to address complicated business challenges.

Preferred Skills:

- Experience by working with Oracle Retail Demand Forecasting which enables him to forecast retail demand and adapt to different retail analytics situations.
- Developed advanced skills in time series forecasting which he applies to retail supply chain demand planning.
- Ensemble modeling techniques which include random forest and boosted tree methods.
- Ability to work with international teams while presenting analytical results for effective communication.
- Analytical abilities together with his statistical skills to develop solutions for complex problems which require creative thinking.

## Consumption Lead (Data Cloud & Agentforce), Salesforce

## Data Visualization Research Specialist, Accenture

## Development Architect Agentic AI, SAP

## AI Practice Director, Google Cloud

# Data Visualization Research Specialist, Accenture

May 19, 2026

Apply Now

## Apply for this job

Email *

Executive Name *

You accept our Terms and Conditions and Privacy Policy

### Job Description

Accenture is hiring for a Data Visualization Research Specialist who will design and develop UI layers for research dashboards and insight platforms. The role requires designers to build user-friendly visual systems which transform difficult data into understandable visual stories and work together with data scientists to produce top-quality analysis results. The team members will develop visual storytelling components together with design systems and AI-based visual analysis tools for enterprise research initiatives.

Qualification: Master’s degree in Design, Computer Science, Statistics, Economics, or related fields

Job Type: Full Time

### Main Duties:

- Design and develop UI layers for dashboards, BI platforms, and research insight systems.
- Analyze user needs and workflows to create intuitive, data-driven visual experiences.
- Creates interactive dashboards and visual storytelling systems through the implementation of current user interface design standards.
- Work with data scientists to maintain visualized insights through accurate and consistent data presentation.
- Develop design systems, UI guidelines, and reusable components for research platforms.

### Essential Qualifications:

- 5 to 10 years of experience in UI development or data visualization roles.
- Advanced proficiency in JavaScript frameworks which include React.js, D3.js, HTML and CSS.
- Experience in creating interactive dashboards and digital platforms which operate on data for practical use.
- Possesses complete knowledge about interaction design and usability testing and accessibility standards and information architecture principles.
- Expertise in converting complex data sets into user-friendly visualizations which executive audiences can easily comprehend.

### Preferred Skills:

- Candidates must have experience with design tools which include Figma and Adobe Illustrator and comparable design platforms.
- Demonstrate Power BI expertise to develop dashboards that support enterprise storytelling.
- Knowledge of Power Apps and Power Automate to create solutions that focus on user experiences.
- Candidate shows interest in GenAI design tools and interactive storytelling advancements.
- Leadership abilities and strong communication skills which enable him to lead teams while fulfilling stakeholder requirements.

# [Remote] AI Development Associate - 6191178

Posted Jun 7, 2026

Note: The job is a remote job and is open to candidates in USA. Accenture is a leading global professional services company that helps organizations build their digital core and drive business transformation. They are seeking an AI Development Associate who will be responsible for fine-tuning Large Language Models and becoming a subject matter expert while actively contributing to team discussions and solutions.

- Responsibilities
- As a LLM Model Developer, resource will be responsible for fine-tuning Large Language Models with an emphasis on instruction fine-tuning and domain adaptation to enhance model relevance and performance in specific contexts
- You are expected to perform independently and become a subject matter expert
- Active participation and contribution in team discussions are required, along with providing solutions to work-related problems
- Expert proficiency in Python (Programming Language) is required
- Advanced proficiency in AI Agents is recommended
- Additionally, advanced proficiency in PostgreSQL and Large Language Models (LLMs) is suggested

- Skills
- Minimum 1 year of work experience
- Minimum 1 year of python experience
- High School Diploma or GED
- Expert proficiency in Python (Programming Language)
- Advanced proficiency in AI Agents
- Advanced proficiency in PostgreSQL
- Advanced proficiency in Large Language Models (LLMs)

- Benefits

highlights:
Generative AI Future of Work Talent Transformation | Accenture
[...]
Reinvented in the age of generative AI
[...]
- Generative AI is likely the most significant change to work since the agricultural and industrial revolutions.
[...]
- Reinventors are breaking out of silos and building trust among their people by actively involving them in redesigning their work and roles.
- We’re bringing data to
[...]
reality we see every day—companies that take a people-centric approach could create $10.3 trillion in economic value.
[...]
Gen AI is already showing broad potential to
[...]
value chain.
[...]
The gen AI revolution is different than anything that’s come before it. Anyone can use it to automate or augment basic tasks, but gen AI is already showing much broader potential to reinvent processes across the entire value chain. To scale this groundbreaking technology responsibly so that work improves for everyone, leaders need to lead and learn in new ways. This means setting and guiding a vision for how to reinvent work, reshape the workforce and prepare workers for a gen AI world, while building a resilient culture to navigate continuous waves of change.
[...]
Gen AI is poised to provide the most significant economic uplift and change to work since the agricultural and industrial revolutions, and will lead to a reinvention of work with more human-centric work processes. Gen AI is democratizing business process redesign, giving everyone—from assembly workers to customer service agents to lab scientists—the power to reshape their own workflows. In fact, our research shows that gen AI offers a trio of opportunities: it can accelerate economic value and drive business growth while also fostering more creative and meaningful work for people.
[...]
Net Better Off at
[...]
- Digital fluency (including cloud, data, security)
- Generative AI fundamentals (including LLMs, architectures, responsible AI principles)
- Ask the right questions
[...]
Rethinking entire workflows provides a clear view of where gen AI can be most impactful, aligning it with business goals for better efficiency and innovation across the enterprise and truly collapsing silos in a lasting, meaningful way. From there, it’s possible to re-focus on how the work needs to change to better serve customers, support your people and achieve business outcomes. New talent models will also be needed to foster a culture where people are shaping their work and how it flows through the organization; as processes change, so does the work. Ensuring that skills and adaptability—of both humans and machines—keep pace with changes across the value chain is crucial.
[...]
Mastering gen AI is not a one-time event—learning must be dynamic. And as use of this technology grows, organizations should further leverage tools and technologies like skill mapping and ontology to facilitate smooth transitions from declining to emerging roles. As work and roles shift, capacity is increased, which frees up time and talent for transitions and/or new role creation that best aligns with strategic customer and business outcomes. This increased capacity is the unlock for the productivity and market share gains CxOs predict will stem from gen AI.
[...]
As organizations integrate gen AI more deeply, they’ll need comprehensive learning initiatives and a strong teach-to-learn culture that presents learning in a three-dimensional way: individual, organizational and the machine itself. After all, humans need to teach the machines (a new skill in and of itself), as both people and the machines

to get better at their

over time to maximize the benefits of gen AI. Appro

so that change is happening with them, not to them. And when individuals are actively

highlights:
Generative AI Technology in Business | Accenture
[...]
- The technology
[...]
ChatGPT will transform work and reinvent business.
[...]
- Advances in language-
[...]
AI – the technology that powers
[...]
like ChatGPT – are ushering in a new era for
[...]
Reinvention.
[...]
The large language models (LLMs) and foundation models powering these advances in generative AI are a significant turning point. They’ve not only cracked the code on language complexity, enabling machines to learn context, infer intent, and be independently creative, but they can also be quickly fine-tuned for a wide range of different tasks.
[...]
We can also expect a large number of new tasks for people to perform, such as ensuring the accurate and responsible use of generative AI systems. It’s why organizations that invest in training people to work alongside generative AI will have a significant advantage.
[...]
Generative AI and LLM applications are ready to consume and easy to access. Companies can consume them through APIs and tailor them, to a small degree, for their own use cases through prompt engineering techniques such as prompt tuning and prefix learning.
[...]
To increase the value of generative AI and foundation models in specific business use cases, companies will increasingly customize pretrained models by fine-tuning them with their own data—unlocking new performance frontiers.
[...]
The coming years will see outsized investment in generative AI, LLMs and foundation models. What’s unique about this evolution is that the technology, regulation, and business adoption are all accelerating exponentially at the same time.
[...]
rapidly evolve –
[...]
and as the
[...]
Cost and carbon emissions are central considerations in adopting energy-intensive generative AI
[...]
Companies must reinvent work to find a path to generative AI value. Business leaders must lead the change, starting now, in job redesign, task redesign and reskilling people. Ultimately, every role in an enterprise has the potential to be reinvented, once today’s jobs are decomposed into tasks that can be automated or assisted and reimagined for a new future of human + machine work.
[...]
Companies will have thousands of ways to apply generative AI and foundation models to maximize efficiency and drive competitive advantage. But they’ll need to reinvent work to find a path to business value from this technology. Business leaders must lead the change, starting now, in job redesign, task redesign and reskilling people.
[...]
Focus on people as much as on technology, ramping up talent investments to address both creating AI and using AI. This means developing technical competencies like AI engineering and enterprise architecture and training people across the organization to work effectively with AI-infused processes.
[...]
Urgently assess whether the company's responsible AI governance regime is sufficiently robust before scaling up generative AI applications. Build in controls for assessing risks at the design stage and embed responsible AI principles and approaches throughout the business.
[...]
Embedded into the enterprise digital core, generative AI and foundation models will optimize tasks, augment human capabilities and open up new avenues for growth. In the process, these technologies will create an entirely new language for enterprise reinvention.
[...]
The TQ is how we build and demonstrate our understanding of transformative technologies and how they deliver on the promise of technology and human ingenuity. From the C-suite to the front line, employees at all levels will need to develop a TQ to drive successful reinvention.
[...]
We invest in continuous training across Accenture, and each employee receives an individual TQ score. Accenture’s TQ learning series is a simple and effective way to ensure every team member learns about technology, how it’s applied, why it matters and how it works with other technologies.

results.title: Understanding Use of Gen AI | Accenture
highlights:
Understanding Use of Gen AI | Accenture
[...]
# Understanding use of gen AI in an AI system
[...]
Understanding AI from a technical perspective will help you learn how to unlock new opportunities, drive innovation, and create lasting value. But with so many terms to understand, what is the best way to approach it? As part of our Demystifying AI series, we have created three short articles that cover the 37 top terms you need to know, with each article focusing on a key AI domain area: learning paradigms, how to build and deploy AI, and gen AI specifics.
[...]
This is the third and final article in our Foundational Understanding blog series, which equips you with a comprehensive understanding of how Ai works. In this article, we explore how various gen AI concepts work together – focusing on types, architecture, and post-deployment details. You can also read our previous articles on understanding AI learning paradigms and building and deploying AI.
[...]
## Large language models (LLMs): Mastering how humans speak
[...]
LLMs as a
[...]
, capable of
[...]
. These AI models have mastered
[...]
language, making them incredibly versatile
[...]
. Whether it
[...]
s translating between languages,
[...]
complex articles,
[...]
or even engaging in creative storytelling
[...]
digital assistant, capable of understanding
[...]
the digital world, and
[...]
essence of an AI agent. They are sophisticated
[...]
programs, equipped with
[...]
sensors" to
[...]
their environment, "brains" (AI algorithms) to
[...]
, and "actuators"
[...]
. From chatbots
[...]
are becoming integral
[...]
our digital lives,
[...]
## LLM serving: Unleashing the power of language models
[...]
Large Language Models (LLMs) possess a remarkable ability to understand and
[...]
human-like text, but their true potential is realized when they're put into action. LLM serving is the process of deploying these powerful models in a way that allows them to efficiently handle requests and generate responses in real-time. Think of it as setting up a high-performance engine to power various AI-driven applications. LLM serving involves making the model accessible through an API or other interface, allowing users or other systems to send prompts and receive responses. This requires careful consideration of factors like latency, throughput, and scalability, ensuring that the model can handle the demands of real-world applications. LLM Serving also involves managing resources efficiently, optimizing the model's performance, and monitoring its behavior to ensure it remains accurate and reliable. By effectively serving LLMs, businesses can unlock a wide range of applications, from chatbots and virtual assistants to content generation and language translation tools, transforming the way they interact with customers and process information.
[...]
ps: Managing large language models
[...]
Just like a living organism, Large Language Models (LLMs) have a lifecycle that requires careful management. LLMOps (Large Language Model Operations) encompasses the practices and tools for effectively managing this lifecycle – from development and deployment to monitoring and continuous improvement. It builds upon the broader principles of MLOps, adapting them to the specific challenges and requirements of working with LLMs. LLMOps streamlines the entire process, enabling efficient collaboration between data scientists, engineers, and operations teams. It involves version control to track changes and experiments, continuous integration and continuous deployment (CI/CD) to automate testing and deployment, and monitoring to track model performance and identify potential issues. It also includes techniques for retraining and updating models to keep them relevant and accurate, as new data becomes available. By implementing LLMOps best practices, organizations can ensure the reliability, scalability, and responsible use of AI, maximizing value and impact.
[...]
Chief AI & Data Officer

results.title: AI-Ready Data for Advanced AI | Accenture
highlights:
AI-Ready Data for Advanced AI | Accenture
[...]
AI-ready data
[...]
of data for the
[...]
- AI momentum is outpacing data-readiness, as siloed,
[...]
data & lack of business context & tacit knowledge inhibit safe, scaled AI adoption.
[...]
- Only 7% surveyed—data reinventors—have progressed far enough in building AI-ready data capabilities required for scaled AI adoption of advanced AI.
[...]
Data reinventors realize significant value, with profit margin uplifts up to 1.6x over industry peers.
[...]
But our analysis shows that only 7%—data reinventors—have reached the level of data-readiness required to scale advanced AI, which includes generative, agentic and physical AI.
[...]
Foundational properties such as completeness, accuracy, consistency, accessibility and governance remain essential but are no longer sufficient. They were built for structured tabular data meant for use by human experts like data scientists and software engineers for reporting and traditional machine learning. Much of this enterprise data is static and siloed. Advanced AI is now exposing limitations such as poor data quality and fragmentation faster than any previous technology.
[...]
To be AI-ready today, organizations still need trusted, governed data that meets the requirements of traditional AI. But advanced AI demands richer context. Data must also serve as a referential corpus drawing from both structured sources and unstructured ones like documents, diagrams and code that capture processes, experience and know-how. It must include the semantic meaning of common terms, entities and their relationships as it applies to a process set as well as comprehensive examples that illustrate use cases. And, increasingly, it must be refreshed in real time.
[...]
While centralizing data in one cloud environment might be most efficient, this is not a realistic ambition, given
[...]
, changing priorities and data sovereignty. It is more pragmatic to aim for
[...]
of data that
[...]
federated.
[...]
for data security, quality and access, while enabling distributed use across
[...]
, using AI and agents to modernize and activate only the data that
[...]
most to priority use cases.
[...]
1. Build a semantic layer, so humans and AI can reason and act using the same business meaning. Domain ontologies, deployed as knowledge graphs, connect data with context in a repeatable and explainable way, helping ensure the right data is used for the right purpose.
[...]
, how and now what beyond systems of record. Context
[...]
, for instance, recall and trace why the
[...]
acted within agentic systems. Systems of record capture the what of the
[...]
: transactions, customers, products, assets and processes. Advanced AI also needs the how, which sits in unstructured knowledge
[...]
, workflows, communications and expert judgment, as well as the now what from real-time signals to respond to change and recommend or initiate the next best action.
[...]
from the AI‑ready data journey
[...]
1. Establish a two-speed value realization model for AI-ready data initiatives. Prove priority AI use cases quickly, in weeks rather than months, using minimum viable data products to test the value case. Once value is demonstrated, use the results to guide deeper investment in data quality, governance, context and reuse so the solution can scale reliably.
[...]
2. Infuse AI into the data lifecycle to accelerate and improve data quality. Use it as the operating layer for managing, governing and activating data end-to-end. Gen AI and agents can automate repetitive tasks such as metadata tagging, lineage mapping, quality monitoring, policy checks and documentation, while shifting human roles toward judgment, validation, exception handling and decision oversight.
[...]
Rewriting platform strategy for agentic AI
[...]
AI is reshaping work and redefining
[...]
. To capture its full value, companies must reinvent platforms and align AI, platform and business strategies.

results.title: Utilizing the Power of Generative AI in High Tech | Accenture
highlights:
Utilizing the Power of Generative AI in High Tech | Accenture
[...]
# Surpass your competition. Utilize the power of generative AI
[...]
## The new days of gen AI
[...]
I'm sure you've read the news about Generative Artificial Intelligence (genAI) technology and how it will revolutionize industries and our everyday life. My daughter is headed to college and her experience in a genAI environment will be completely different from my days in school, so I’ll focus this topic on something I do know . . . how High Tech companies can do business with genAI. I want to explore the benefits of genAI technology and a few examples I've seen in the industry.
[...]
### Unlock the power of generative AI technology
[...]
The power of Generative AI technology is revolutionizing how High Tech companies do business, enabling organizations to quickly and efficiently automate processes, which can free up time for more strategic activities while optimizing resources.
[...]
GenAI is becoming a priority for companies, in the latest Accenture Tech Vision research, we saw that 96% of high tech executives are either very or extremely inspired by the new capabilities offered by AI foundation models. In my conversations with industry leaders, I could imagine genAI supporting these potential areas:
[...]
- Predict analytics
[...]
### Case studies of companies leveraging generative AI
[...]
GenAI has been used by high tech companies to gain a competitive edge. The High Tech industry is in the unique, circular position of creating the devices that use the technology and then applying that tech into their development. By increasing the accuracy of predictions, these businesses have seen increased efficiency and cost savings while providing personalized customer experiences. To show how successful generative AI can be, let's look at some case studies across the industry.
[...]
Built on their oneAPI platform, Intel collaborated with Accenture to create a set of 34 open-source AI reference kits to help Enterprise developers and data scientists deploy AI at a faster rate. The kits include model code, training data and instructions for machine learning and oneAPI components. The kits can streamline process of imbedding AI into applications which can reduce the workflow time compared to traditional model development workflows.
[...]
These case studies demonstrate how effective genAI can be when employed correctly and highlight the opportunities available for high tech companies who want to gain an advantage over their competition. Understanding the technology and studying successful implementations is essential if you want to make the most of it within your own organization.
[...]
## Getting started with generative AI: What you should know
[...]
Embarking on a journey with genAI can be daunting, yet high tech firms can reap the rewards from this powerful technology by properly preparing. Let me break it down:
[...]
1. Build a model or leverage a partnership that can help implement a model that meets your company's needs of selecting an appropriate AI foundation and architecture.
2. Data collection should be done thoughtfully as it is crucial to obtaining reliable results.
3. After testing and validating the model in development, deployment into production will allow customers and other stakeholders access to accurate predictions about customer behavior or trends hidden within datasets.
[...]
I invite you to read the Accenture Tech Vision report, which dives into this topic in further detail. GenAI provides high tech businesses with an opportunity to become more efficient and accurate and excel in better customer experiences. To make the most out of this technology, it’s important that businesses understand how it works. With careful planning and execution of these steps, an organization can unlock valuable insights and personalized experiences better than your competition.
[...]
Managing Director – Strategy & Consulting, High Tech, Data & AI, Global

results.title: Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
highlights:
Optum Hiring Data Scientist | Jobformore
[...]
# Data Scientist
[...]
Optum is seeking a Data Science professional who is passionate about applying machine learning, Generative AI, and Large Language Models (LLMs) to solve complex healthcare problems on a scale. Imagine working with massive volumes of healthcare data spanning clinical, claims, behavioral, and digital interactions – powering intelligent personalization, discovery, and decision support for millions of members, providers, and care teams.
[...]
At Optum, the Data Science mission is to transform healthcare data into robust, scalable AI solutions, including LLM based and Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) systems that combine enterprise knowledge with advanced language models to deliver accurate, contextual, and trustworthy insights. These solutions help uncover deep usage patterns, member journeys, and care signals that inform and continuously evolve Optum’s digital products, clinical platforms, and healthcare services.
[...]
Your work will directly influence how healthcare consumers engage with digital experiences, how clinicians access timely insights, and how business and care strategies are shaped through data. By joining Optum’s data science team, you will be part of an exciting journey to build advanced ML and GenAI driven products, grounded in responsible AI principles, that operate at enterprise scale and create meaningful impact across the healthcare ecosystem.
[...]
- Design, develop, and deploy advanced analytics, machine learning, and Generative AI solutions to address complex healthcare and business problems using largescale structured and unstructured data
[...]
- Applies advanced techniques including statistics
[...]
- Build and optimize LLM based applications, including Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) frameworks, to enable accurate, context aware, and explainable insights from enterprise healthcare data sources such as clinical notes, claims, provider data, and policy documents
[...]
- Develop, evaluate, and finetune machine learning and GenAI models, including prompt engineering, retrieval strategies, and model evaluation techniques to improve accuracy, relevance, and performance
[...]
- Translate complex analytical and GenAI outputs into
[...]
- Support prototyping, validation, and deployment of GenAI and ML solutions, working with engineering teams to ensure solutions are scalable, secure, and maintainable. Continuously stay current with advancements in LLMs, GenAI frameworks, and healthcare AI trends, contributing innovative ideas and improvements to Optum’s capabilities
[...]
### Required Qualifications:
[...]
- Bachelor’s Degree (preferably in information technology, engineering, math, computer science, analytics, engineering or other related field)
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in handling/active development in data science projects
[...]
- 4+ years of experience in Microsoft Azure Cloud, Databricks, Mlflow, Spark SQL & Python
[...]
- Experience using Machine Learning Algorithms: Linear & Logistic Regression, Time Series analysis, Support Vector Machine, K-Means clustering, K-Nearest Neighbors Decision Trees, Random Forest, Naive Bayes, PCA, SVD, Artificial Neural Networks, Association Rules, Genetic Algorithm, Optimization, Bagging and Boosting algorithms etc.
[...]
- Understanding of data, schema, data model, machine learning and how to bring efficiency in big data related life cycle
[...]
- Proven skills to create opportunities using machine learning or artificial intelligence
[...]
Optum, part of UnitedHealth Group, is a global health services and technology company revolutionizing healthcare delivery through data analytics, pharmacy benefits, and digital health solutions that connect millions to better care. Headquartered in Eden Prairie, Minnesota, Optum serves patients, providers, payers, and pharmacies worldwide with innovative platforms for care optimization, claims processing, and population health management.
